_meta.method: ultracode workflow: 10 doc readers over 30 official agent-sdk pages + changelog + npm 0.3.199, 918 inventory items, 1 synthesis agent auditing actual src
_meta.weighting: FULL=1 PARTIAL=0.6 ACCEPTED=0.15 MISSING=0 over P0+P1 rows, N/A-BY-DESIGN excluded

matrix.official: query({prompt, options}): Query async generator; primary entry point
matrix.gap: Both prompt modes implemented; unified input queue; direct Messages API engine instead of CLI subprocess

matrix.official: tool(name, desc, zodShape, handler, extras?: {annotations: ToolAnnotations}); Zod 3 and 4
matrix.gap: No 5th extras param — ToolAnnotations typed on SdkMcpToolDefinition but never attachable/populated; zod v4 only

matrix.official: {name, version?, tools?} -> McpSdkServerConfigWithInstance in-process server
matrix.gap: In-process dispatch, zod-validated handlers, Map semantics for dup names

matrix.official: Pre-warm CLI subprocess before prompt exists; AsyncDisposable handle
matrix.gap: No subprocess spawn/init cost exists in direct-API design; first request is already cheap. A no-op shim could be added for type compat

matrix.official: Extract embedded native binary from bun-compiled executable
matrix.gap: No bundled native binary; pure-JS package works inside bun compile directly

matrix.official: ({dir?, limit?, includeWorktrees?}) over ~/.claude store, sorted lastModified desc
matrix.gap: Reads own JSONL store only; option bag is {sessionDir} not {dir, limit, includeWorktrees}; no worktree awareness

matrix.official: Returns SDKSessionInfo | undefined
matrix.gap: Returns null instead of undefined; own store only; SDKSessionInfo lacks tag/gitBranch

matrix.official: (sessionId, {dir?, limit?, offset?}) -> SessionMessage[]
matrix.gap: Not exported; transcript is readable via internal store.load() but no public message-level API

matrix.official: Append custom-title/tag entries, latest wins
matrix.gap: Absent; SDKSessionInfo.customTitle typed but nothing writes it

matrix.official: Alpha: resolve effective settings with CLI merge engine, provenance, sources
matrix.gap: No settings engine at all

matrix.official: Session-storage page: standalone functions accepting sessionStore option
matrix.gap: None exported; fork exists only as query option

matrix.feature: npm bundled native binary + platform packages + bun compile flow

matrix.gap: The removal of the binary is the product thesis; nothing to bundle

matrix.official: AbortController for cancelling operations
matrix.gap: Outer signal wired through queue, transport, tools, hooks

matrix.official: Extra directories Claude can access
matrix.gap: Enforced via resolveWithin containment in fs tools (no symlink-escape handling, documented)

matrix.official: Agent for main thread; must exist in agents or settings
matrix.gap: Not typed, not in ACCEPTED warn list — silently ignored with no warning

matrix.official: Record<string, AgentDefinition>; drives Agent tool

matrix.official: Required gate for permissionMode bypassPermissions
matrix.gap: bypassPermissions works WITHOUT this flag — the official safety interlock is absent

matrix.official: Auto-approve list; does not restrict availability; glob restrictions on MCP allow rules

matrix.official: Bare name removes tool from context; scoped rule denies matching calls in every mode; * / mcp__* globs in deny position
matrix.gap: Scoped deny works in all modes incl. bypass; but bare name does NOT remove the tool definition from the model request (model still sees and attempts it); deny-position globs '*'/'mcp__*' unsupported

matrix.official: SdkBeta[] enable beta features
matrix.gap: string[] forwarded/merged into anthropic-beta header (looser type than SdkBeta[])

matrix.gap: Invoked only on prompt-fallthrough (correct), updatedInput/updatedPermissions honored; but suggestions and requestId never passed, and null return is treated as deny instead of skip-response semantics

matrix.official: Continue most recent conversation in cwd
matrix.gap: Resumes latest session from own store, not scoped by cwd, cannot see Claude Code CLI sessions

matrix.gap: Wired to tools, containment, session meta

matrix.official: CLI-embedded OTel instrumentation configured via env
matrix.gap: No telemetry emission of any kind; would need first-party reimplementation in the engine

architectureDelta: Context compaction: ARCHITECTURE.md has no compaction model at all (auto-compact near context limit, manual /compact, microcompaction, compact_boundary emission, PreCompact firing, CLAUDE.md compactor guidance) — the loop simply accumulates history until the API rejects it

Subagent runtime: no design for the Agent tool, AgentDefinition execution, background-by-default subagents (v2.1.198), 5-level nesting, context isolation, parent_tool_use_id threading, subagent transcript files, or agent resume

Background task framework: run_in_background Bash, Monitor (command/WebSocket event sources), TaskOutput/TaskStop, task_started/progress/updated/notification stream messages, stall watchdog — entirely absent from the module map
Tool search / deferred MCP schema loading (ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH auto:N, MCPSearch) — architecture always ships every schema on every request and never discusses the context-cost tradeoff
File checkpointing: no checkpoint store, rewindFiles, RewindFilesResult, files_persisted event, or checkpoint-UUID (replay-user-messages) design

SessionStore external mirroring: dual-write architecture, append/load/listSubkeys/delete adapter contract, batched/eager flush, mirror_error retry semantics, InMemorySessionStore, conformance suite — ARCHITECTURE.md only knows its own local JSONL store

Structured outputs: outputFormat json_schema, final-output validation + retry loop, structured_output result field, error_max_structured_output_retries, model-fallback retraction — no engine hook exists for any of it

Settings engine: settingSources loading (CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json rules + shell hooks, .mcp.json, skills, output styles), local>project>user precedence, flag-settings layer, applyFlagSettings, resolveSettings, managed/server-managed policy tiers

Skills, plugins, slash commands, output styles: the entire filesystem-feature ecosystem (SKILL.md discovery, Skill tool, SdkPluginConfig loading, command dispatch, $ARGUMENTS) is out of scope of the module map

Sandboxing: SandboxSettings (bubblewrap/socat, network/filesystem allowlists), dangerouslyDisableSandbox permission fallback, sandbox startup-failure result semantics, and Bash AST permission parsing — our Bash design is plain spawn with prefix-string rules

Observability: the 33-variant SDKMessage stream (tool_progress, permission_denied, rate_limit_event, informational, hook lifecycle events, prompt_suggestion...) and the whole OTel surface (spans, TRACEPARENT propagation) are unmodeled — our stream has 7 variants
Automatic prompt caching strategy: no cache_control breakpoint placement or excludeDynamicSections-style cache-key hygiene; every turn resends full-cost input

WarmQuery/startup() pre-warm lifecycle and reinitialize()/pending_permission_requests control-protocol redelivery: correctly moot for in-process design, but the tradeoff is never stated in ARCHITECTURE.md

Permission extras: 'auto' classifier mode, ask rules as a first-class evaluation step, defer flow (deferred_tool_use + resume), CLAUDE_SDK_CAN_USE_TOOL_SHADOWED warning — the gate contract documents a 9-step order that deviates from the official 6-step order (allow rules before mode; plan-mode write routing)

Third-party providers and runtime env knobs: Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry switches, API_TIMEOUT_MS, CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_RETRIES, retry watchdog, stream idle watchdog — transport design covers only direct Anthropic API with its own provider config

V2 session API removal (createSession send/stream removed in TS SDK 0.3.142): migration surface our docs never mention; irrelevant to implement but COMPAT.md should note it to preempt swap-in questions
User-input surfaces: AskUserQuestion flow (canUseTool interception, answers/updatedInput protocol, toolConfig previews) and MCP elicitation (onElicitation) have no design treatment

Session interop: official on-disk layout (~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>, CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, cleanupPeriodDays) vs our incompatible ~/.bpt-agent store — the interop decision is implicit, never argued
compatMdCorrections: canUseTool 'FULL' overstates: gate.ts never passes suggestions or requestId to the callback, and a null return is treated as deny — official semantics for null are 'app already sent the control_response externally; skip response', so should be PARTIAL

allowedTools/disallowedTools 'FULL' overstates: (1) bare-name disallowedTools does NOT remove the tool definition from the model request (loop.ts builds toolDefs from builtinTools unfiltered by deny rules; official removes it before evaluation); (2) allow rules evaluate before permission mode, so in plan mode an allow rule auto-approves write tools — official says file edits are NEVER auto-approved in plan; (3) '*'/'mcp__*' deny-position globs unsupported — should be PARTIAL

Hooks table 'Notification | PARTIAL | fired for permission denials only in v0.1' is factually wrong: no code path in src/ fires the Notification event at all (grep confirms only type definitions). Should be ACCEPTED (never fires)

Hooks table '`defer` | UNSUPPORTED | treated as deny with warning' is inaccurate: runner.ts has no defer branch; an unrecognized permissionDecision silently matches no aggregation case, so the call falls through and may still be ALLOWED by rules/mode — more permissive than documented, not a deny
PostToolBatch 'FULL' overstates: our input is { tool_names: string[] }, official is { tool_calls: [{ tool_name, tool_input, tool_use_id, tool_response? }] } — should be PARTIAL
MessageDisplay 'FULL' overstates: fires once per completed message with { message_text }; official fires per delta with { turn_id, message_id, index, final, delta } — should be PARTIAL

SDKMessage 'result ... FULL' overstates: success arm lacks api_error_status/ttft_ms/ttft_stream_ms/structured_output/deferred_tool_use/terminal_reason/fast_mode_state/origin; error arm uses errorMessage: string instead of official errors: string[] — should be PARTIAL

The budget result subtype 'error_max_budget (BPT extension)' is mislabeled: the official SDK has a real subtype 'error_max_budget_usd'; ours shadows it under a different name, so drop-in consumers switching on the official name silently miss budget stops. This is a naming bug, not an extension
system/init 'FULL' overstates: missing claude_code_version, betas, skills, plugins fields; slash_commands hardcoded [] — should be PARTIAL

tool() 'FULL' overstates: no 5th extras parameter, so ToolAnnotations can never be attached (the annotations field on SdkMcpToolDefinition is dead); docs also promise Zod 3 + 4, we support v4 only — should be PARTIAL

'maxThinkingTokens / thinking | FULL' overstates: maxThinkingTokens alone never enables thinking (used only as budget fallback when thinking.type==='enabled'), and ThinkingConfig uses budget_tokens/budget instead of the official camelCase budgetTokens (type-level break); display unsupported — should be PARTIAL
PreToolUse/PostToolUse 'FULL' should note input-shape gaps: hook input objects lack tool_use_id (official carries it in the input as well as the 2nd arg) and PostToolUse lacks duration_ms

UserPromptSubmit 'FULL — block stops the turn' understates: a block yields error_during_execution and ends the ENTIRE query, including in streaming-input mode where the official CLI keeps the session alive
mcpServerStatus() 'FULL' overstates: McpServerStatus lacks the official config/scope/tools fields (per-server tool listing unavailable) — should be PARTIAL

COMPAT.md omits four official Options fields entirely — agent, settings, permissionPromptToolName, extraArgs. None is typed nor in ACCEPTED_OPTION_KEYS, so passing them is silently ignored with NO debug warning, contradicting the stated ACCEPTED contract ('each present key emits exactly one debug warning')

supportedModels()/initializationResult() PARTIAL rows should add a type-break note: ModelInfo is { id, displayName? } while the official shape keys the model as 'value' (+ description, supportsEffort, etc.) — consumers reading .value get undefined
Minor type deviations worth listing: ApiKeySource has 'none' instead of official 'oauth'; PermissionUpdateDestination lacks 'cliArg'; SlashCommand lacks argumentHint/aliases; AgentInfo lacks model
